sentences.text: I. Medical Necessity Aetna considers the use of quantitative EEG (brain mapping), also known as BEAM (Brain Electrical Activity Mapping), medically necessary only as an adjunct to traditional EEG for any of the following: A. For ambulatory recording of EEG to facilitate subsequent expert visual EEG interpretation; or B. For continuous EEG monitoring by frequency-trending to detect early, acute intracranial complications in the operating room or intensive care unit (ICU); or C. For evaluation of certain members with symptoms of cerebrovascular disease whose neuroimaging and routine EEG studies are not conclusive; or D. For evaluation of dementia and encephalopathy when the diagnosis remains unresolved after initial clinical evaluation; or E. For screening for possible epileptic seizures in high-risk ICU members; or F. For screening for possible epileptic spikes or seizures in long-term EEG monitoring; or G. For topographic voltage and dipole analysis in pre-surgical evaluations for intractable epilepsy. II.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven A. Aetna considers the use of quantitative EEG experimental, investigational, or unproven for all other indications, including any of the following diagnoses because there is inadequate scientific evidence to prove its clinical usefulness for these indications: Alcoholism Anxiety As a marker of central neuropathic pain intensity in spinal cord injury As a prognostic biomarker for long-term outcomes in preterm infants with neurological and medical complications Asperger syndrome and other autism spectrum disorders Attention disorders (including attention deficit hyperactivity disorder) Bipolar disorder Chronic pain (diagnosis and guide to strategies for pain control) Depressed mood after stroke (screening) Depression (including monitoring and predicting the response of treatment) Drug abuse Eating disorder Fibromyalgia Hypoxic ischemic encephalopathy Insomnia Learning disability Management of individuals after chimeric antigen receptor (CAR)-T cell therapy Management of individuals in coma Mild or moderate head injury Minimally conscious state/persistent vegetative state Monitoring therapy responses and prognosticating neurological recovery of individuals with COVID-19 Obsessive compulsive disorder Panic disorder Parkinson's disease (including use as a biomarker in non-invasive brain stimulation therapy in Parkinson’s disease) Post-concussion syndrome Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) Prediction of clinical impairment in stroke Prediction of outcomes in children following cardiac arrest Predicting response to psychotropic medication Prion diseases Schizophrenia Sepsis-associated encephalopathy prognosis Sports concussion (diagnosis and assessment of recovery) Tinnitus.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: B. BrainScope One system (Ahead 300) is considered experimental, investigational, or unproven for evaluation of concussion / traumatic brain injury because of insufficient evidence. III.
sentences.label: CLAIM

Z73.819 Behavioral insomnia of childhood Personal history of transient ischemic attack (TIA), and cerebral infarction without Z86.73 residual deficits [depressed mood after stroke] Z86.74 Personal history of sudden cardiac arrest Quantitative EEG (qEEG) is a method of analyzing the electrical activity of the brain to derive quantitative patterns that may correspond to diagnostic information and/or cognitive deficits.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Quantitative EEG, a technique for topographic display and analysis of brain electrophysiological data, has been proposed for use in the diagnosis of various psychiatric disorders.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Clinical studies have demonstrated distinctive forms of brain electrical activity in psychiatric conditions including attention deficit disorder, schizophrenia, major depression, and obsessive-compulsive disorder.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: However, the clinical significance of these distinctive patterns of brain wave activity is unknown.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Thus the role of quantitative EEG in diagnosis, evaluation of disease progression, and treatment of these conditions has yet to be elucidated.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: A report from the American Academy of Neurology and the American Clinical Neurophysiology Society concluded that quantitative EEG remains investigational for clinical use in post-concussion syndrome, mild-to-moderate head injury, learning disability, attention disorders, schizophrenia, depression, alcoholism, and drug abuse.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Clinical studies have demonstrated distinctive forms of brain electrical activity in neurologic and psychiatric conditions including learning disabilities, autism, traumatic brain injury, coma, schizophrenia, major depression, and obsessive-compulsive disorder.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: However, the clinical significance of these distinctive patterns of brain wave activity is unknown.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Thus the role of quantitative EEG in diagnosis, evaluation of disease progression, and treatment of these conditions has yet to be elucidated.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Quantitative EEG has been proposed for use in a broad array of potential applications.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: There are no current guidelines from leading medical professional organizations recommending the use of quantitative EEG as a screening test for neurological and psychiatric conditions.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In addition, there are no peer-reviewed published prospective studies of the use of quantitative EEG screening for these conditions showing that management is altered such that clinical outcomes are improved.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: There are no published clinical studies demonstrating that use of quantitative EEG reduces the number of imaging studies or other follow-up tests.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In addition, there are no current guidelines from leading medical professional organizations recommending the use of quantitative EEG either as a prerequisite to, or as a replacement for, imaging studies.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: While there is some evidence that electroencephalograph activity differs between normal control subjects and subjects suffering from tinnitus, additional evidence is needed to evaluate the value of including quantitative EEG in a battery of electrophysiological tests for the clinical identification of a predominantly central type of tinnitus.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In addition, there is little evidence to support the use of quantitative EEG to determine the need for change of medications in the treatment of tinnitus.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: However, there is a lack of reliable evidence from prospective studies demonstrating that clinical outcomes are improved by basing selection of psychotropic medications on quantitative EEG results compared to empiric selection.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The FDA approved prescribing information for psychotropic medications includes no recommendation for use of quantitative EEG in selection or dosing, and there are no current guidelines from leading medical professional organizations recommending such use of quantitative EEG.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The authors concluded that qEEG measures of background rhythm frequency and relative power in the band are potential predictive biomarkers for dementia incidence in PD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that qEEG analysis reveals significant differences between FM patients compared to age- and gender-matched healthy controls in a normative database, and has the potential to be a clinically useful tool for assessing brain function in FM patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the CHI/b identified neonates with high Sarnat scores and abnormal EEG.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested a disrupted connection of the brain's entire functioning of maniac patients and represented a deregulation in cortical inhibitory mechanism.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors stated that these findings need to be confirmed in larger samples and in bipolar patients before start the pharmacological treatment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that emerging technologies and novel approaches that aid in sports concussion diagnosis and management are being introduced at a rapid rate.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that abnormalities in EEG and EPs are present in the majority of septic patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the basic diagnostic evaluation for differentiating PVS from MCS consists of a standardized clinical examination.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) The American Academy of Neurology (AAN)‘s practice advisory on “The utility of EEG theta/beta power ratio in ADHD diagnosis” (Gloss et al, 2016) evaluated the evidence for EEG theta/beta power ratio for diagnosing, or helping to diagnose ADHD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors identified relevant studies and classified them using AAN criteria.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that it is unknown whether a combination of standard clinical examination and EEG theta/beta power ratio increases diagnostic certainty of ADHD compared with clinical examination alone.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Level R (Level R recommendations are ones that “the guideline authors assert should be applied only in research settings).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors stated that these findings indicated that children with ADHD did not neurophysiologically constitute a homogenous group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors believed that these qEEG subtypes of ADHD are expected to provide valuable information for accurately diagnosing ADHD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that increased alpha and theta power at spontaneous EEG and low amplitudes of event- related potential (ERP) during various stimuli appeared to be clinical characteristics of individuals with chronic pain; qEEG can be a simple and objective tool for studying the mechanisms involved in chronic pain, identifying specific characteristics of chronic pain conditions and providing insights about appropriate therapeutic approaches.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors noted that since they did not aim to analyze or discuss the clinical significance of EEG as a tool to detect changes after interventions, their findings and conclusions came from observational studies.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors found lower main background frequency, α power and α/θ power ratio and higher θ power in patients compared to control participants.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings supported the use of qEEG to follow the progression of prion disease, with potential to help evaluate the treatment effects in future clinical-trials.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors stated that continued follow-up of aIPD patients and retrospective analysis of converting clinical cases may be helpful.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the aberrant EEG oscillation in subjects with PSD differed between subjects with lesions of the left and right hemispheres, suggesting a complex association between depression and lesion location in stroke patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that depressed mood after stroke was associated with functional status; and qEEG parameters may be a useful tool in timely screening for depressed mood after stroke.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these were the first available normative data for these tests when administered using the Ahead 300 system and will assist health care professionals in determining the degree to which scores on the cognitive tests reflect impaired performance.

sentences.text: The authors concluded that using an EEG-based biomarker high accuracy of predicting the likelihood of being CT+ was obtained, with high NPV and sensitivity to any traumatic bleeding and to hematomas; specificity was significantly higher than standard CT decision rules.

sentences.text: The authors concluded that rapid triage of TBI relies on strong initial predictors.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that despite substantial methodological and analytical differences across the 16 studies, the current review suggested that rs-EEG may provide a reliable technique to identify persistent functional changes in athletes after a concussion.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors found that for patients with or without symptoms of abnormal cognition or behavior, current evidence does not support the clinical use of qEEG either at the time of the injury or remote from the injury to diagnose mTBI (level U).

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the findings of this study suggested that high-trait-anxiety individuals failed to synchronize DMN during RS, reflecting a possible top-down cognitive control deficit.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the findings of this study suggested that the increase in delta power in the central region and left P3 was negatively correlated with the decrease in executive function.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that qEEG features can standardize EEG interpretation and predict neurologic outcomes in children following cardiac arrest.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors found that spectral EEG frequencies were independent predictors of performance in attention tasks.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that neonatal quantitative EEG measures may be used as prognostic biomarkers to identify those infants who will develop long-term difficulties and who might benefit from early interventions.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that qEEG features at baseline and reactivity could prognosticate neurological recovery in critically ill patients with COVID-19.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The ubiquity of cEEG monitoring allows rapid translation in the clinical practice to facilitate decision-making to mobilize or withhold limited resources, guide patient selection, and planning adaptive clinical trials.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the results of this analysis indicated that the SARS-CoV-2 virus causes changes in the nervous system that can be manifested and detected in the EEG record.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that based on these findings, they recommended that additional studies are needed to validate EEG as a biomarker in non- invasive brain stimulation therapy in patients with PD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that they systematically evaluated major findings on qEEG and stroke, evidencing strengths, and potential pitfalls of this promising branch of research.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that EEG could be used to detect the intensity of NP to serve as a surrogate or pharmacodynamic marker.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In addition, the authors stated that future longitudinal studies with a larger number of subjects should be able to reveal additional smaller effect sizes relationships between pain and EEG, which could not be detected in the study.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that EEG was useful in seizure diagnosis; and qEEG has a possible role as a specific biomarker of encephalopathy/neurotoxicity.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that alpha-coma demonstrated increased aperiodic activity and EEG complexity, despite similar alpha power and clustering coefficient.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the use of qEEG spectral analysis to monitor MDD patients provided valuable insights into treatment effectiveness.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The distinct patterns of cortical activity observed across most brain regions before treatment, compared to HC, highlighted the potential of qEEG to predict treatment outcomes.
